params.name: Milo of Croton
params.description:
On Nov 14, 1682 AD, Pierre Puget completed this marble sculpture.
Milo of Croton is a sculpture born in seventeenth-century France, created by the sculptor Pierre Puget. The statue depicts the scene of Milo being bitten by a lion: Milo was a Greek wrestler who had won many Olympic championships, but in his old age, he tried to test his strength by trying to split a tree trunk with his bare hands, only to get stuck in it and be killed by a lion.
